one_time_description: Loss on long-term investments: $4,922,265 write-down of Tahltan Heritage Trust investment and $8,000,000 loss on transfer of Volcano Creek Limited Partnership to THT for nil consideration
economic_notes: Band operates economic development including tourism, post office, equipment operations, community economic development. Tahltan Band Development Corporation subsidiaries operate a grocery store and gas bar (Tahltan Centre Ltd.) and a seasonal motel and restaurant (Stikine River Song Cafe and General Store Ltd.) in Telegraph Creek, BC. TNDLP has joint venture agreements providing support and construction services to various industries. Wildfire (Alkali Lake) significantly impacted community beginning August 2018 with ongoing rebuilding efforts at March 31, 2020. BCFN Gaming Revenue Sharing LP revenue of $933,670 received in 2020.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED with multiple bases: (1) Stikine River Song Cafe & General Store Ltd. and Tahltan Centre Ltd. recorded under modified equity instead of full consolidation; (2) No financial information received for Tahltan Nation Development Limited Partnership (33.3% interest) so no income/loss recorded; (3) Tahltan Heritage Trust accounting changed from modified equity to cost method prospectively with insufficient audit evidence; (4) Volcano Creek Limited Partnership transferred to THT for nil — uncertain accounting treatment; (5) Tahltan Nation Community Development Corporation dissolved with no records available; (6) Tangible capital assets listing is incomplete and may contain assets no longer in use. Auditor firm name not provided in extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + FNHA + DFO + CMHC + Ottawa Trust + Ministry of Forests + Ministry of Children + Ministry of Justice = $11,270,624. OSR includes insurance proceeds, donations, BCFN Gaming, rental, Tahltan Central Government, SWWG, Canada Post, AHMA, FNESC. Long-term debt includes revolving facility/loans payable $1,578,319 plus obligations under capital lease $100,614. Annual debt service estimated from repayment of revolving facility and loans ($495,230) plus capital lease repayment ($16,585) = $511,815. Large non-cash losses of $11,588,725 from long-term investments drove the overall deficit. Wildfire ongoing with ~$0.7M insurance proceeds and cleanup costs subsequent to year-end.
